canonical_definition: Based on the available data, ASAP is characterized as a dataset used for evaluating automated scoring systems. The single provided source refers to it as The Hewlett Foundation Short Answer Scoring (ASAP) dataset. It is described as containing short essay responses from students. The subject matter of these responses is specified to be within the domains of science and biology.
papers: CBP-Tuning: Efficient Local Customization for Black-box Large Language Models
Hallucination Detection inLLMs Using Spectral Features of Attention Maps
KGECalibrator: An Efficient Probability Calibration Method of Knowledge Graph Embedding Models for Trustworthy Link Prediction
Mind the Value-Action Gap: DoLLMs Act in Alignment with Their Values?

definitions.text: The Hewlett Foundation Short Answer Scoring (ASAP) dataset, which contains short essay responses on science and biology topics for evaluating automated scoring systems.
definitions.paper: Mind the Value-Action Gap: DoLLMs Act in Alignment with Their Values?
